sentence: The students put ____ books on the desk.
label: their / there / they're

label: there / their / they're

sentence: ____ always late to every meeting without fail.

label: its / it's

sentence: ____ raining very heavily outside today.
label: it's / its

sentence: Is this ____ umbrella or did someone leave it behind?
label: your / you're

sentence: I cannot believe ____ late for the third time this week.
label: you're / your

sentence: She owns ____ dogs and a very playful cat.
label: two / too / to

sentence: The jacket was ____ expensive for me to afford.
label: too / two / to

sentence: The ____ was bitterly cold throughout the entire winter.
label: weather / whether

sentence: She could not decide ____ to accept the job offer abroad.
label: whether / weather

sentence: Please come over ____ and take a look at this.
label: here / hear

sentence: He had to ____ a formal letter of apology to the principal.
label: write / right / rite

sentence: You can ____ the mountains very clearly from this viewpoint.
label: see / sea

label: sea / see

sentence: She pressed the ____ hard to avoid the oncoming vehicle.
label: brake / break

sentence: Please do not ____ the vase — it is a priceless antique.
label: break / brake

sentence: He walked on the ____ floor without wearing any slippers.
label: bare / bear

sentence: A grizzly ____ emerged suddenly from the dense forest.
label: bear / bare

sentence: Every citizen deserves ____ treatment under the law.
label: fair / fare

sentence: The bus ____ has increased significantly this year.
label: fare / fair

sentence: She had long and flowing ____ that reached well past her waist.
label: hair / hare / heir

sentence: I bought a brand new ____ of shoes for the wedding ceremony.

sentence: The white ____ billowed beautifully in the ocean breeze.
label: sail / sale

sentence: The grandmother told a fascinating fairy ____ to the children.
label: tale / tail

sentence: She mixed the ____ carefully with water to prepare the dough.

sentence: The rabbit disappeared quickly into a ____ in the ground.
label: hole / whole

sentence: The two nations signed a treaty to restore lasting ____.
label: peace / piece

sentence: The brave ____ rode through the dark forest alone.
label: knight / night

sentence: The important ____ arrived three days later than expected.
label: mail / male

sentence: They arranged to ____ at the coffee shop at noon.

sentence: She climbed every ____ slowly as she was recovering from surgery.
label: stair / stare

sentence: The old bridge was reinforced with thick beams of ____.
label: steel / steal

sentence: The bark of the ancient oak tree was rough and covered in moss.
answer: the outer covering of a tree trunk
options: the outer covering of a tree trunk
the sharp sound made by a dog
a type of small wooden boat
label: bark

sentence: He swung the bat with great force and hit the ball out of the park.
answer: a wooden or metal club used to hit a ball
options: a wooden or metal club used to hit a ball
to blink or flutter the eyelids rapidly
a turn at hitting in cricket
label: bat

sentence: They sat on the grassy bank and watched the river flow peacefully by.
answer: the sloping land alongside a river
options: the sloping land alongside a river
a financial institution that holds money
to tilt an aircraft during a turn
to save or store something for later
label: bank

sentence: The feather was so light that it floated gently in the still air.
answer: not heavy; having very little weight
options: not heavy; having very little weight
brightness produced by a source of illumination
a source of fire used to ignite something
pale or not dark in colour
label: light

sentence: He struck a match and lit the candle on the dinner table.
answer: a short stick used to produce fire by friction
options: a short stick used to produce fire by friction
a sports competition between two teams
to pair things that go well together
something that closely resembles another thing
label: match

sentence: The cherry blossoms bloomed spectacularly in the spring sunshine.
answer: the season between winter and summer
options: the season between winter and summer
a coiled metal device that stores mechanical energy
to jump or leap forward suddenly
a natural source of water rising from the ground
label: spring

sentence: A massive wave crashed against the rocky coastline with tremendous force.
answer: a ridge of water moving across the surface of the sea
options: a ridge of water moving across the surface of the sea
a gesture of greeting using an outstretched hand
a widespread trend or movement across society
a disturbance that carries energy through a medium
label: wave

sentence: She always preferred plain food without any spices or strong sauces.
options: a large area of flat and level land
obvious or easy to see and understand
to complain or express dissatisfaction aloud
label: plain

sentence: The pianist performed the entire sonata in the key of C major.
answer: a musical scale forming the basis of a composition
options: a musical scale forming the basis of a composition
a metal instrument used to open a lock
the most important or essential element of something
a low-lying coral island or reef
label: key
